titles.*: So you want to be a DBA?

*: Day-to-day life of a DBA. Tips and tricks from a DBA's POV to developers.

titles.*: Job title: UX/UI designer and developer @ Codeborne
Train your design eye (UI design eye)

*: Most developers are already making design decisions every day — every padding value, every color choice, every div placement.
But without knowing the underlying rules, it's hard to tell what's off and even harder to fix it.
Design taste isn't a gift. It's a set of logical, learnable rules — and once you know them, your eye starts catching things automatically.
This talk walks you through the four rules that come up in almost every design review: alignment, spacing, sizing, and color.
Each one is explained with real before/after examples, exaggerated on purpose so the difference is impossible to miss.
By the end you'll have a framework you can apply immediately — whether you're reviewing your own work, giving feedback, or prompting AI to fix something specific.
The developers who get consistently great UI aren't the ones with the most design talent. They're the ones who know what to look for.
